title: Seeing the Multivariate Normal
tagline: From one bell curve to clouds, marginals, and slices
summary: A narrative explainer that starts with the ordinary bell curve, then builds a 2D Gaussian through examples, sampling, parameter learning, marginals, and conditional slices.
highlights: Start with a live 1D bell curve before moving into the 2D cloud.
See how covariance turns a round blob into a tilted ellipse and how eigenvectors reveal its natural axes.
Sample actual points from a multivariate normal and watch the estimates wobble from draw to draw.
Fit a Gaussian back to toy datasets by estimating the mean vector and covariance matrix directly from points.
Use synced marginal and conditional views to see what gets forgotten and what becomes sharper after observing one coordinate.
Finish with a compact set of takeaways that tie together geometry, probability, and conditioning.
